ddt:Target.Name: MFD v1.8
ddt:Target.Description:
******************** HISTORY ********************
v0.1 July 2010 : Creation based on NFA
v0.2 November 2010 : Update based on MFD_DiagnosisServiceImplementation 1.0 and DDT database of NFA2.5
V0.3 November 2010 : Update DTC list
V0.4 January 2011 :
V0.5 January 2011 : Add Eco function configuration
V0.6 May 2011 : Update based on MFD_DiagnosisServiceImplementation 1.6
V0.7 May 2011 : Update based on MFD_DiagnosisServiceImplementation 1.7
V0.8 May 2011 : Deletting duplicated requests and Updating based on MFD_DiagnosisServiceImplementation 1.8
V0.9 Jun 2011 : Update based on MFD_DiagnosisServiceImplementation 2.0
V1.0 Jul 2011 : Modification of CNF_LEARN & CNF_CONSO requests (unchek of variable length option)
V1.1 Aug 2011 : Add X61 applicability
V1.2 Aug 2011 : Replace X61 applicability by X61 ², And correction of HVAC_CNF parameter values.
V1.7 Dec 2011: Addition of L38VE, X91 ph3 and X07 EDISON in vehicle type configuration parameter VEHTYP_CNF.
V1.3 September 2011 : Adding X87 applicability
V1.4 October 2011 : Update based on MFD_DiagnosisServiceImplementation 2.5
V1.5 November 2011 : Correction of PartNumber.BasicPartList, HardwareNumber.BasicPartList, ApprovalNumber.BasicPartList
V1.6 November 2011 : Deletion of the DTC : "Circuit Current Above Threshold" for USB 3 (TCU)
V1.7 December 2011 ; Addition of L38VE, X91 ph3 and X07 EDISON in vehicle type configuration parameter VEHTYP_CNF. Addition of Renault Sport Configuration in ECU configuration Screens
V1.8 January 2012 : WRITE ALL button sends all the configuration parameters (including ECO parameter, Tracability parameters, Phone parameters...)

Item.Text: Device and failure type ODB codding
circuit short to ground or open
circuit short to battery or open
signal rate of change below threshold
signal rate of change above threshold
signal level out of range/zero adjustment failure
signal has too few transitions / events
signal has too many transitions / events
alive / sequence counter incorrect / not updated
value of signal protection calculation incorrect
Component | system operation obstructed or blocked
test completed OK : default not present
Default not yet checked by the ECU
Default detected, confirmed but not more present
Request for availibility LID from 0 to 19
Request for availibility LID from 21 to 39

Data.Name: CANMMUX.Ref Messagerie
Data.Comment: version édition du document décrivant la messagerie CAN-M

Item.Text: Cable attached with IEC 62196-2 type 1 connector
Cable attached with IEC 62196-2 type 2 connector mode 1-3
Cable attached with IEC 62196-2 type 2 connector mode 2-3
Cable attached with IEC 62196-2 type 3 connector 16 A-mono
Cable attached with IEC 62196-2 type 3 connector 32 A-mono
Cable attached with IEC 62196-2 type 3 connector 32 A-triphasé

Data.Name: Kit AssemblyReference
Data.Comment: Kit assembly reference (identical to part number)

Item.Text: Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Turned since beginning of monitoring cycle
Turned since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Turned since beginning of monitoring cycle
Pressed since beginning of monitoring cycle
Turned since beginning of monitoring cycle

Data.Name: ECUR.reset typeR
Data.Comment: Type of reset asked by the tool

Data.Name: ECUR.reset typeS
Data.Comment: Type of reset asked by the tool

Data.Name: LID.VIN.CRC
Data.Comment: key code confirming the validity of the VIN, written at Renault factory

Data.Name: LID.VIN.VIN
Data.Comment: Serial number of the car, written at Renault factory

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: StartAfterSalesSession
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: DataWrite.Consumption_config_values
Request.Comment: Comsumption data to be filled during the project

Request.Name: DataWrite.Learning_Profile
Request.Comment: Comsumption data to be filled during the project

Label.Text: - force configuration to be taken into account
- reset configuration parameters to default values
- except for part reference, kit assembly reference, last after-sales operation date, driving restriction
test parameters reset is used to:
- reset the value of mesured parameters for MSM or CSW buttons test and for BT phone test
ID #10: Z.E. type 2 (round socket) cable max 22kW
ID #13: Z.E. type 2 (round socket) cable max 3kW
ID #15: Z.E. type 2 (round socket) cable max 43kW
PHONE ACOUSTIC PARAMETERS : This parameter is used to configure the bluetooth phone.
CNF_PACT1 (first part of phone acoustic parameter)
CONSUMPTIONCONFIGURATION : This parameter is used to configure the comsumption computation feature .
LEARNING PROFILE : This parameter is used to configure the learning profile feature .
This parameter is used to configure the comsumption computation feature .
This parameter is used to configure the learning profile feature .
Vehicle speed displayed by MFD is processed from wired information (pulses) coming from ABS/ESC ECU.
The GPS reception level is processed by the MFD.
MSM buttons values, from CAN, are used by MFD as control commands. This screen is used to test the MSM command integration.
Instantaneous CSW buttons values maintained pressed during 2s if no others actions

Button.Text: Reset memorized value of MSM buttons

Label.Text: CSW buttons values, from CAN, are used by MFD as control commands. This screen is used to test the CSW command integration.
instantaneous CSW buttons values maintained pressed during 2s if no others actions

Button.Text: Reset memorized value of CSW buttons

Label.Text: The data of the last connected bluetooth device processed by the MFD.
To perform the measurement, unconnect all bluetooth device thought MFD screen, then connect one bluetooth device.
N - production number of the day
This routine displays a permanent black screen:
This routine displays a permanent white screen:
This routine displays a cyclic black-white screen sequence (3 times):
If display does not turn back to normal after STOP, please perform a HW reset.
If display does not turn back to normal after STOP, please perform a HW reset.
If display does not turn back to normal after STOP, please perform a HW reset.
This routine is used to check rear view camera functionality.
This routine displays rear view picture.
This routine is used to calibrate rear view camera guidelines settings.
If display does not turn back to normal after STOP, please perform a HW reset.
This routine is used to test MFD guidance voice interruptions.
MFD sends a tone to mono output.
This routine is used to test MFD main source.
MFD sends a tone to stereo output, alternatively on right and left loudspeakers .
This routine is used to test microphone.
MFD generate a sound on the stereo output and check if the sound is captured by the microphone.
